_comment: WC2026 Group H, MD3. RECALIBRATED with team news (~confirmed XIs; GK 'Muslera' likely a search error, ignore). URUGUAY missing two KEY starters to injury: Ronald Araujo (best CB -> defense weaker) AND De Arrascaeta (main creator -> less chance creation); Darwin Nunez STARTS (Bielsa picks him over Vinas) -> more clinical up top, which OFFSETS the Arrascaeta loss on attack but not the Araujo loss on defense. SPAIN at full strength: Lamine Yamal STARTS (recovered) -> elite right wing vs Uruguay LB Juan M. Sanabria. Net: Spain MORE favoured (Uruguay leakier without Araujo, Spain at full power). Modest adjustment (confirmed team news only). Home = Uruguay, away = Spain. Cards not modelled.

meta.stakes: Spain (4 pts) near-through and at full strength (Lamine Yamal starts). Uruguay (2 pts) must win but are without Araujo (CB) and Arrascaeta (creator); Darwin Nunez starts. Crucial; Spain a clearer favourite than the pre-XI read.

home.name: Uruguay
notes: Darwin Nunez STARTS (clinical) but lost creator Arrascaeta -> attack ~neutral. attack 1.05, context 1.05 (must-win).
Without Araujo (best CB) the back line is weaker vs an elite Spain attack. defense 1.12.
Needs to win -> open game, rho -0.05.

away.name: Spain
notes: Elite and at FULL strength: Lamine Yamal starts (recovered). attack 1.50.
Clearer favourite now that Uruguay miss Araujo + Arrascaeta; lifted in line with the market.

_lineups.Uruguay: Muslera (GK, confirmed); WITHOUT Ronald Araujo (CB) and De Arrascaeta (creator), both injured; Darwin Nunez STARTS.
_lineups._note: All key items confirmed -> recalibration locked: Uruguay 23.8 / Draw 25.3 / Spain 50.8 (MEDIUM). lambdas 1.08 / 1.68 unchanged from the team-news recalibration.

_postmatch.result: Spain 1-0 Uruguay (Uruguay 0-1 Spain). Spain controlled, clean sheet; Uruguay blanked.
_postmatch.model_1x2: Uruguay 23.8 / Draw 25.3 / Spain 50.8 (MEDIUM, after XI recalibration)
_postmatch.verdict: Clean hit and the XI recalibration was VINDICATED. The team-news move (Spain up to 50.8% at full strength with Lamine; Uruguay down without Araujo + Arrascaeta) was right: Spain won by the model's #2 scoreline (0-1, 10.1%), kept a clean sheet (34%), and Uruguay -- missing their creator Arrascaeta -- failed to score, exactly the depleted-attack read. Controlled 1-0, Under 2.5.
_postmatch.lesson: Confirmed-XI adjustments (Araujo+Arrascaeta out, Lamine in) are the LEGITIMATE kind of recalibration -- they paid off, unlike the speculative motivation tweaks. Trust real team news.
